## Introduction

Agricultural Robotics Statistics: As for the agricultural sector, [agricultural robotics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agricultural_robot) allows for reaping the benefits of increased efficiency, productivity, and sustainability. The global agricultural robotics industry is also witnessing a considerable upsurge due to developments in AI, automation, and robotics, which have become an important aspect of modern agriculture.

The global agricultural robotics industry has grown substantially, with the market reaching an estimated USD 14.74 billion in 2024 and poised to expand further due to rapid advances in artificial intelligence, automation, and precision technologies. Forecasts suggest this sector could exceed USD 26 billion by 2032 at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of around 18 percent. Other projections indicate potential growth to between USD 86 and 103 billion by 2032–33, with CAGR estimates ranging from 20.5 percent to 25.7 percent.

In 2023, hardware, such as milking robots, drones, and driverless tractors, accounted for over 55 percent of the sector, while milking systems alone held nearly 49 percent of the total market. Meanwhile, autonomous guidance systems are already used on more than 50 percent of major field crops like corn, soybeans, and wheat in the United States.

These trends underline how robotics and AI-driven automation are significantly improving efficiency, productivity, and sustainability in modern agriculture. Below is a detailed analysis of the Agricultural robotic statistics, growth drivers, and inclusion in the future outlook.

## Conclusion

The agricultural robotics industry is a vital component of the future of farming, addressing labor shortages, reducing costs, and boosting yields. Agricultural robotic statistics estimated a market value of $25.7 billion by 2030 and a strong annual growth rate.

Agricultural robotics is set to transform farming practices globally. Technological advancements, government support, and private sector investments are driving this growth, making robotics more accessible and affordable.

### Sources

### FAQ.

The global agricultural robotics market is on a high growth trajectory, with a projected revenue of USD 25.7 billion by 2030. This upsurge is attributable to the incorporation of artificial intelligence, automation, and robotics in farming, which in turn cuts labour costs and increases efficiencies in agriculture.

Harvesting robots, seeding machines, welding robots, and drones are some of the most utilised agricultural robots. In 2023, harvest robots held close to 35% of the market share, particularly in applications that involved growing fruits and vegetables where the demand for local sustainable food is high.

Regional adoption varies quite dramatically: In the US, for example, 45% of large-scale farms have adopted the use of agricultural robots, including drones and harvesting robots. In Europe, this share is around 37%, and in the Asia Pacific region, which consists of farms in China and Japan, approximately 28% of the farms incorporate robotics due to labour and technology costs.

The agricultural drone sector is expected to expand from $4.5 billion in 2023 to $17.9 billion in 2028, registering a growth rate of 31.5% in annual earnings. These drones assist in high-tech agriculture by providing information, monitoring the status of crops, and creating aerial maps of farmlands, thus aiding in sustainable agricultural practices.

The financing of agricultural robotics will grow to $2.8 billion in 2023, with significant origins from the governmental side. For instance, $500 million was given by the U.S. Department of Agriculture to encourage the adoption of new technologies in the agricultural sector. Dominant companies in the segment are making large investments as well, such as John Deere and DJI, making sophisticated robotics less of a challenge for most farmers.

# Global Agriculture Robots Research Report 2023-2028: Explore the Unmanned Future of Farming - Driverless Tractors, Drones and Precision Robots Redefining the Burgeoning Market

![Research and Markets Logo]( "Research and Markets Logo")

## News provided by

Aug 25, 2023, 12:00 ET

## Share this article

![](data:image/jpg;base64,)

Share this article

![](data:image/jpg;base64,)

DUBLIN, Aug. 25, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The ["Agriculture Robots Market by Type (Unmanned Aerial Vehicles/Drones, Milking Robots, Driverless Tractors, Automated Harvesting Systems), Farming Environment (Indoor and Outdoor), End-use Application and Region - Global Forecast to 2028"](https://www.researchandmarkets.com/reports/5853940/agriculture-robots-market-type-unmanned-aerial?utm_source=CI&utm_medium=PressRelease&utm_code=t646t5&utm_campaign=1881994+-+Global+Agriculture+Robots+Research+Report+2023-2028%3a+Explore+the+Unmanned+Future+of+Farming+-+Driverless+Tractors%2c+Drones+and+Precision+Robots+Redefining+the+Burgeoning+Market&utm_exec=chdo54prd) report has been added to **ResearchAndMarkets.com's** offering.

The agricultural robots market is projected to grow from USD 13.5 Billion in 2023 to USD 40.1 Billion by 2028, at a CAGR of 24.3%.

Increased awareness about digital agriculture is driving the widespread usage of agriculture robots as farmers recognize the transformative potential of advanced technologies in modernizing and optimizing farming practices. Digital agriculture encompasses the integration of cutting-edge technologies such as robotics, artificial intelligence, data analytics, and the Internet of Things (IoT) into traditional farming methods, unlocking new possibilities for increased efficiency, sustainability, and productivity.

One of the key drivers of agriculture robot adoption is the growing understanding of the benefits they offer. With increased awareness, farmers are recognizing that these robots can automate labor-intensive tasks, reduce operational costs, and enhance overall farm management. By leveraging advanced sensors and AI algorithms, agriculture robots can collect real-time data on soil health, crop conditions, and weather patterns, enabling data-driven decision-making and precision farming practices.

Agricultural robots automate slow, repetitive, and dull tasks for farmers, allowing them to focus more on improving overall production yields. They include unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) or drones, milking robots, automated harvesting systems, driverless tractors, and other robots, for instance, unmanned ground vehicle (UGV) robots, used in nurseries or greenhouses, sorting and packing robots, and weed control robots.

Drones are expected to lead the agricultural robots market; however, with the high rate of commercialization of driverless tractors, they are expected to overtake drones within the next three to four years. The driverless tractors segment is expected to have the maximum growth potential among agricultural robot types.

The agricultural robots market in Europe exhibits a high degree of professionalism and technological adoption. However, the lack of large farms and slightly higher input costs create a marginal disadvantage compared to the US market. The agricultural robots market in Europe is projected to witness substantial growth in the near future as this region is currently in the early stage of the adoption of autonomous harvesting systems and driverless tractors.

Prominent companies include DJI (China), PrecisionHawk (US), Trimble Inc. (US), Parrot Drones (France), AeroVironment, Inc. (US), Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. (Japan), AgEagle Aerial Systems, Inc. (US), DroneDeploy (US), 3DR (US), and Sentera Inc. (US) among others.

**The Outdoor segment is expected to account for the largest share in 2023.**

Agriculture robots are set to revolutionize outdoor farming applications, offering numerous benefits that will transform the way farming is conducted. These advanced machines, equipped with cutting-edge technologies, are poised to address critical challenges faced by farmers and enhance productivity, sustainability, and efficiency in outdoor farming.

Agriculture robots can perform tasks with unmatched precision, thanks to their advanced sensors, GPS technology, and artificial intelligence capabilities. They can accurately plant seeds, apply fertilizers, and dispense pesticides, reducing waste and optimizing resource usage. Precision farming ensures that crops receive the right amount of inputs precisely where they are needed, leading to increased yields and cost savings.

**The Farm produce sub-segment is projected to dominate the market share in the end-user segment during the forecast period.**

Agricultural robots will revolutionize farm produce by enhancing efficiency and productivity. These robots can autonomously perform various tasks, such as planting, weeding, harvesting, and monitoring crops. With precise data collection and analysis, they optimize resource usage and reduce waste.

Additionally, robots can operate 24/7, ensuring timely actions, even in adverse conditions. Their consistent performance improves crop quality and yield. By minimizing manual labor, farmers save time and costs, enhancing their profitability. Moreover, agricultural robots promote sustainable practices by using fewer chemicals and reducing environmental impact. Overall, these technological advancements empower farmers to meet rising demands and secure a more food-secure and sustainable future.

Driverless tractors are expected to be utilized on a large scale - despite their high price - as labor costs keep rising. Various AGVs are expected to be utilized for farming field crops for planting, spraying, and weeding. Since field crops require extensive farmlands, UAVs are also expected to be utilized on a large scale in field crops compared with other types of agricultural produce.

Hence, field crops are expected to have the highest share of the market and the highest growth rate during the forecast period.

**Europe is to grow significantly during the forecast period.**

According to the European Committee of Farm Machinery Manufacturer's Associations (CEMA), the European agricultural machinery market is currently growing, which is expected to fuel the growth of the agricultural robots market as well. The major factors contributing to the agricultural robots market's growth in Europe include improved productivity through mechanization, an optimized supply chain, and increasing labor cost owing to the shortage of skilled labor. Uncertainty regarding government support, low farm income, and import restrictions from Russia are the major restraints.

The European agricultural machinery industry is one of the most developed in the world and is supported by the presence of global players, such as John Deere (US), Small Robot Company (UK), Earth Rover (UK), Saga Robotics (Norway), CNH Industrial (The Netherlands), and AGCO Corporation (US).

# Market.Biz: Agricultural Robots Market to Hit USD86.5B by 2033

## Growing cost of labor encouraging agricultural robots automation

PUBLISHED ON **January 23, 2024**

![Agricultural Robots Market](https://www.morningagclips.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Agricultural-Robots-Market-1-720x400.jpg)

![Agricultural Robots Market](https://www.morningagclips.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Agricultural-Robots-Market-1-720x400.jpg)

Robotic technology in agriculture holds vast untapped potential, offering advanced solutions from livestock management to precision farming and environmental protection. (Courtesy photo)

WASHINGTON — The [Agricultural Robots market](https://market.us/report/agricultural-robot-market/) is projected to expand from **USD 13.4 billion in 2023** to an estimated **USD 86.5 billion by 2033**, reflecting a compound annual growth rate **(CAGR) of 20.5%** over the forecast period.

The agricultural robot market is experiencing a significant upsurge, primarily driven by the increasing demand for automation in agriculture. This growth can be attributed to several key factors. Firstly, the rising cost of labor in the agricultural sector is compelling farmers and agribusinesses to turn towards automation as a cost-effective solution. The deployment of robots in farming tasks not only reduces labor costs but also enhances efficiency, precision, and productivity.

Additionally, the growing global population and the consequent rise in food demand necessitate innovative approaches to boost crop yields and farming efficiency. Agricultural robots, with their ability to perform tasks such as planting, weeding, and harvesting more efficiently and with minimal human intervention, are emerging as pivotal tools in meeting these escalating food production demands.

Furthermore, technological advancements are playing a crucial role in the expansion of the Agricultural Robot market. The integration of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and big data analytics in agricultural robots has led to the development of more sophisticated and intelligent farming solutions. These technologies enable precision farming, where robots can analyze soil conditions, monitor crop health, and provide data-driven insights for better crop management. The increasing emphasis on sustainable farming practices is also propelling the market growth.

Agricultural robots equipped with sensors and GPS technology can optimize the use of resources like water, fertilizers, and pesticides, thereby reducing the environmental impact of farming. Government initiatives and funding in various regions, particularly in developed countries, are further bolstering the market by supporting research and development in agricultural robotics. All these factors collectively underscore the booming nature of the Agricultural Robot market, positioning it as a key component in the future of smart and sustainable farming.

## Key Takeaways

* **Market Growth Projection:** The Agricultural Robots Market is projected to expand from USD 13.4 billion in 2023 to approximately USD 86.5 billion by 2033, achieving a CAGR of 20.5%.
* **Primary Product Types:** Milking Robots, holding a 48.6% market share in 2023, are leading the market, followed by rapidly growing segments like drones and driverless tractors.
* **Offering Dynamics:** Hardware, accounting for over 55% of the market, includes automated tractors and drones, while software and services are crucial for their functionality and efficiency.
* **Application Insights:** Planting and Seeding represent a key segment, focusing on precision, with Soil Management expected to grow rapidly due to advancements in mobile robots for fertilizing and weeding.
* **Market Drivers and Restraints:** The market is driven by labor shortages and rising costs, promoting efficiency, but is challenged by the high initial costs of digitization, particularly for smaller farms.
* **Untapped Opportunities:** Livestock management robotics, utilizing IoT and remote sensing, presents significant potential, alongside drones and robotic technology that streamline farming.
* **Challenges in Full Automation:** Complete automation in agricultural robots faces challenges of complexity and cost, with autonomous tractors and inspection robots not fully operational until post-2025.
* **Regional Analysis:** North America leads in drone sales due to favorable regulatory policies, while the EU’s targeted pesticide application regulations via robots indicate potential market growth.
* **Key Players and Recent Developments:** Companies like AGCO Corporation and John Deere are at the forefront, innovating in precision spraying and route planning technologies.

![](https://www.morningagclips.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Agricultural-Robot-Market-Size-Forecast.jpg)

The Agricultural Robots Market is anticipated to surge to Around USD 86.5 billion by 2033. (Courtesy image)

## Regional Analysis

### **North America**

* **Drone Sales:** North America, particularly the United States, leads in drone sales, representing about 37% of the global market. The region’s adaptability to new technologies significantly contributes to this dominance.
* **Regulatory Influence:** The Federal Aviation Administration’s (FAA) favorable policy changes towards commercial drone use have catalyzed the market’s growth.
* **Agricultural Benefits:** Drones are extensively utilized for their imaging capabilities, enhancing crop yield and profitability in the agricultural sector.
* **Technological Infusion:** The integration of advanced technologies in farming practices has significantly improved the profitability and production efficiency in the agricultural sector of developed countries in North America.

### European Union

* **Government Support:** Increased government backing for modern agricultural techniques, including initiatives like the Farm Advisory System (FAS), is driving market demand. The FAS plays a crucial role in helping farmers understand and comply with EU regulations related to the environment, animal health, and good agricultural practices.
* **Regulatory Framework for Drones:** The EU has established comprehensive rules and regulations governing drone operations, fostering a conducive environment for the growth of the agricultural robot market.
* **Venture Capital and Industrialization:** The increasing flow of venture capital into agricultural technology and the ongoing industrialization of agriculture in the region are expected to positively impact segment demand, promoting market growth.
* **Focus on Sustainable Practices:** The EU’s emphasis on sustainable and environmentally friendly farming practices is likely to further bolster the adoption of agricultural robots, aligning with regional policies and market trends.

## Driver

**Growing Cost of Labor Encouraging Automation**

* The surging global population and escalating labor costs are key drivers of the agriculture robots market.
* Automation through agricultural robots addresses challenges of boosting production amidst limited labor availability and rising expenses, enhancing productivity, efficiency, and precision.
* In regions like California, where farm wages are high, robots offer a cost-effective solution for labor-intensive tasks, enabling skilled workers to focus on more strategic activities.
* Major corporations are investing in agricultural robotics startups, indicating strong market support and growth potential.

## Restraint

**High Cost of Digitization for Small Farms**

* The significant investment required for agricultural robots presents a major barrier, especially for small and medium-sized farms.
* The high costs lead to a digital divide in agriculture, where larger farms benefit from technological advancements, leaving smaller farms behind.
* This financial challenge limits the development of affordable agricultural technology and restricts access for budget-conscious farmers.
* Addressing this issue requires collaborative efforts from governments, industry players, and research institutions to develop cost-effective solutions and provide financial support for adoption.

## Opportunity

**Untapped Market Potential and Scope for Automation in Agriculture**

* Despite widespread use in food processing, agricultural robotics is less prevalent but holds significant untapped potential, especially in livestock management with technologies like IoT tags and remote sensing.
* The integration of drones and robotics in farming, for tasks like precise plowing and crop spraying, presents significant growth opportunities.
* Predictions indicate a shift towards robotic milking and diversified tasks in livestock management, highlighting the sector’s potential.
* EU regulations favoring robots for targeted pesticide application and the less stringent rules on autonomous agricultural vehicles indicate an expanding market scope.

**Find additional highlights on the growth strategies adopted by vendors and their product offerings: [Ask For Sample](https://market.us/report/agricultural-robot-market/request-sample/)**

## Challenge

**High Cost and Complexity of Fully Autonomous Robots**

* Current technologies for fully autonomous agricultural robots, such as tractors and fruit-picking robots, face challenges in deployment due to high costs and technical complexity.
* The steep price range of these robots makes them inaccessible for many farmers, though leasing options are being explored.
* Advances in technologies like soft robotics and sensing are needed to enhance the feasibility of these robots.
* Industry experts predict the operational viability of fully autonomous agricultural robots to be likely post-2025, indicating a need for continued innovation and investment in this area.

## Type Analysis

* **Milking Robots:** Dominated the agricultural robot market in 2023, with over 48.6% market share. These robots have revolutionized dairy farming by automating the milking process, enhancing efficiency, and improving herd management.
* **Drones:** Significant growth encouraged by increasing venture funding. Widely used in agriculture for capturing videos of harvesting systems, planting, and field scouting, thereby driving segment demand.
* **Driverless Tractors:** Expected to witness substantial growth, especially in row and broad-acre crop farming. Growth is driven by the industrialization of agriculture globally, including in countries like India and China.

## By Offering

* **Hardware:** Accounted for over 55% of the market share in 2023. Includes physical robots such as automated tractors, drones, and robotic arms used in various agricultural operations.
* **Software:** Plays a pivotal role in the agricultural robot sector, serving as the control center for these robots. Enables functionalities like data analysis, crop monitoring, and autonomous navigation.
* **Services:** Includes maintenance, repair, and support, ensuring the continuous operation and efficiency of these high-tech agricultural robots. This segment provides farmers with necessary technical assistance and expertise.

## Application Analysis

* **Planting and Seeding:** Emerged as a significant segment in 2023, holding over 24.6% of the market share. Robots in this category specialize in precision planting and seeding, optimizing crop distribution, and minimizing seed wastage.
* **Soil Management:** Anticipated to be the fastest-growing segment due to the increasing use of mobile robots for tasks like fertilizing and weeding.
* **Crop Management:** Mainly utilized by medium to small-sized farms and in horticulture. Growth in this segment is driven by increasing farmer awareness and software solutions for field surveys and data analytics.
* **Field Farming:** Venture funding for UAV manufacturers and growing farmer awareness of drone benefits are expected to drive this segment. Drones are increasingly used for disease detection and health monitoring in crops, helping to reduce crop damage.

URL: https://cdn7.slideserve.com/12618083/agriculture-robots-global-market-report-2023-n.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A drone is flying over a cultivated field with rows of yellow and green plants, illustrating the adoption of agriculture robots in 2023.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://app.11press.com/storage/6Yw2yFGK3xkXufJof6fXlk3e2VZls14WgGWfvS1j.png
DESCRIPTION: The bar chart illustrates the rapid growth in global agriculture robot sales from 2022 to 2033, with projections showing sales increasing from approximately 3,689 million USD in 2022 to nearly 39,629 million USD in 2033.

URL: https://www.acumenresearchandconsulting.com/reportimages/Website-PR-Image_Global-Agriculture-Robots--Drones-Market.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The infographic shows that the global market for agricultural robots and drones is projected to reach USD 13.7 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 20.1% from 2024 to 2032, with North America being the largest region and the Asia-Pacific region the fastest-growing market.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: The agri-robotics market is projected to grow from USD 13.4 billion in 2023 to USD 86.5 billion by 2033, reflecting a CAGR of 20.5%.
Agricultural robots enhance efficiency and reduce labor costs, markedly outperforming traditional farming methods in precision, yield, and sustainability.
Adoption rates vary: 45% in the U.S., 37% in Europe, and 28% in Asia-Pacific, driven by local labor costs and technological investments.
